applications.description: Returns a list of summary details for all the applications in a runtime environment.

nextToken.description: A pagination token that's returned when the response doesn't contain all applications.

applicationArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the application.

creationTime.description: The timestamp when the application was created.

deploymentStatus.description: Indicates either an ongoing deployment or if the application has ever deployed successfully.

engineType.description: The type of the target platform for this application.

environmentId.description: The unique identifier of the runtime environment that hosts this application.

lastStartTime.description: The timestamp when you last started the application. Null until the application runs for the first time.

roleArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the role associated with the application.

versionStatus.description: Indicates the status of the latest version of the application.

nextToken.description: If there are more items to return, this contains a token that is passed to a subsequent call to this operation to retrieve the next set of items.

engineType.description: The type of target platform for the application.

engineVersion.description: The version of the engine type used by the application.

environments.description: Returns a list of summary details for all the runtime environments in your account.

nextToken.description: A pagination token that's returned when the response doesn't contain all the runtime environments.

creationTime.description: The timestamp when the runtime environment was created.

engineType.description: The target platform for the runtime environment.

environmentArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of a particular runtime environment.

environmentId.description: The unique identifier of a particular runtime environment.

instanceType.description: The instance type of the runtime environment.

networkType.description: The network type supported by the runtime environment.

applicationArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the application.

taskId.description: The task identifier. This operation is asynchronous. Use this identifier with the GetDataSetExportTask operation to obtain the status of this task.
The task identifier. This operation is asynchronous. Use this identifier with the GetDataSetImportTask operation to obtain the status of this task.

environmentId.description: The unique identifier of the runtime environment.

applicationArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the application.

creationTime.description: The timestamp when this application was created.

deployedVersion.description: The version of the application that is deployed.

engineType.description: The type of the target platform for the application.

environmentId.description: The identifier of the runtime environment where you want to deploy the application.

kmsKeyId.description: The identifier of a customer managed key.

lastStartTime.description: The timestamp when you last started the application. Null until the application runs for the first time.

listenerArns.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) for the network load balancer listener created in your Amazon Web Services account. Amazon Web Services Mainframe Modernization creates this listener for you the first time you deploy an application.

listenerPorts.description: The port associated with the network load balancer listener created in your Amazon Web Services account.

loadBalancerDnsName.description: The public DNS name of the load balancer created in your Amazon Web Services account.

logGroups.description: The list of log summaries. Each log summary includes the log type as well as the log group identifier. These are CloudWatch logs. Amazon Web Services Mainframe Modernization pushes the application log to CloudWatch under the customer's account.

roleArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the role associated with the application.

tags.description: A list of tags associated with the application.

targetGroupArns.description: Returns the Amazon Resource Names (ARNs) of the target groups that are attached to the network load balancer.

creationTime.description: The timestamp when the application version was created.

definitionContent.description: The content of the application definition. This is a JSON object that contains the resource configuration and definitions that identify an application.

batchJobIdentifier.description: The unique identifier of this batch job.

endTime.description: The timestamp when the batch job execution ended.

executionId.description: The unique identifier for this batch job execution.

jobId.description: The unique identifier for this batch job.

jobStepRestartMarker.description: The step/procedure step information for the restart batch job operation.

returnCode.description: The batch job return code from either the Blu Age or Micro Focus runtime engines. For more information, see Batch return codes in the IBM WebSphere Application Server documentation.

startTime.description: The timestamp when the batch job execution started.

status.description: The status of the batch job execution.

fileBatchJobIdentifier.description: Specifies a file associated with a specific batch job.

fileName.description: The file name for the batch job identifier.

folderPath.description: The relative path to the file name for the batch job identifier.

restartBatchJobIdentifier.description: Specifies the required information for restart, including executionId and JobStepRestartMarker.

executionId.description: The executionId from the StartBatchJob response when the job ran for the first time.

jobStepRestartMarker.description: The step/procedure step information for a restart batch job operation.

fromProcStep.description: The procedure step name that a batch job was restarted from.

fromStep.description: The step name that a batch job was restarted from.

skip.description: The step-level checkpoint timestamp (creation or last modification) for an Amazon Web Services Blu Age application batch job.

stepCheckpoint.description: Skip selected step and issue a restart from immediate successor step for an Amazon Web Services Blu Age application batch job.

toProcStep.description: The procedure step name that a batch job was restarted to.

toStep.description: The step name that a batch job was restarted to.

s3BatchJobIdentifier.description: Specifies an Amazon S3 location that identifies the batch jobs that you want to run. Use this identifier to run ad hoc batch jobs.

bucket.description: The Amazon S3 bucket that contains the batch job definitions.

identifier.description: Identifies the batch job definition. This identifier can also point to any batch job definition that already exists in the application or to one of the batch job definitions within the directory that is specified in keyPrefix.

fileName.description: The name of the file that contains the batch job definition.

scriptName.description: The name of the script that contains the batch job definition.

keyPrefix.description: The key prefix that specifies the path to the folder in the S3 bucket that has the batch job definitions.

scriptBatchJobIdentifier.description: A batch job identifier in which the batch job to run is identified by the script name.

scriptName.description: The name of the script containing the batch job definition.

blocksize.description: The size of the block on disk.

creationTime.description: The timestamp when the data set was created.

dataSetOrg.description: The type of data set. The only supported value is VSAM.

lastReferencedTime.description: The last time the data set was referenced.

lastUpdatedTime.description: The last time the data set was updated.

location.description: The location where the data set is stored.

recordLength.description: The length of records in the data set.

gdg.description: The generation data group of the data set.

limit.description: The maximum number of generation data sets, up to 255, in a GDG.

rollDisposition.description: The disposition of the data set in the catalog.

po.description: The details of a PO type data set.

encoding.description: The character set encoding of the data set.

format.description: The format of the data set records.

ps.description: The details of a PS type data set.

encoding.description: The character set encoding of the data set.

format.description: The format of the data set records.

vsam.description: The details of a VSAM data set.

alternateKeys.description: The alternate key definitions, if any. A legacy dataset might not have any alternate key defined, but if those alternate keys definitions exist, provide them as some applications will make use of them.

cacheAtStartup.description: If set to True, enforces loading the data set into cache before it’s used by the application.

compressed.description: Indicates whether indexes for this dataset are stored as compressed values. If you have a large data set (typically > 100 Mb), consider setting this flag to True.

encoding.description: The character set used by the data set. Can be ASCII, EBCDIC, or unknown.

primaryKey.description: The primary key of the data set.

length.description: A strictly positive integer value representing the length of the primary key.

offset.description: A positive integer value representing the offset to mark the start of the primary key in the record byte array.

recordFormat.description: The record format of the data set.

kmsKeyArn.description: The identifier of a customer managed key used for exported data set encryption.

statusReason.description: If dataset export failed, the failure reason will show here.

summary.description: A summary of the status of the task.

failed.description: The number of data set exports that have failed.

inProgress.description: The number of data set exports that are in progress.

pending.description: The number of data set exports that are pending.

succeeded.description: The number of data set exports that have succeeded.

total.description: The total number of data set exports.

summary.description: A summary of the status of the task.

failed.description: The number of data set imports that have failed.

inProgress.description: The number of data set imports that are in progress.

pending.description: The number of data set imports that are pending.

succeeded.description: The number of data set imports that have succeeded.

total.description: The total number of data set imports.

creationTime.description: The timestamp when the deployment was created.

environmentId.description: The unique identifier of the runtime environment.

actualCapacity.description: The number of instances included in the runtime environment. A standalone runtime environment has a maximum of one instance. Currently, a high availability runtime environment has a maximum of two instances.

creationTime.description: The timestamp when the runtime environment was created.

engineType.description: The target platform for the runtime environment.

environmentArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the runtime environment.

environmentId.description: The unique identifier of the runtime environment.

highAvailabilityConfig.description: The desired capacity of the high availability configuration for the runtime environment.

instanceType.description: The type of instance underlying the runtime environment.

kmsKeyId.description: The identifier of a customer managed key.

loadBalancerArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) for the load balancer used with the runtime environment.

name.description: The name of the runtime environment. Must be unique within the account.

networkType.description: The network type supported by the runtime environment.

pendingMaintenance.description: Indicates the pending maintenance scheduled on this environment.

preferredMaintenanceWindow.description: The maintenance window for the runtime environment. If you don't provide a value for the maintenance window, the service assigns a random value.

publiclyAccessible.description: Whether applications running in this runtime environment are publicly accessible.

securityGroupIds.description: The unique identifiers of the security groups assigned to this runtime environment.

status.description: The status of the runtime environment. If the Amazon Web Services Mainframe Modernization environment is missing a connection to the customer owned dependent resource, the status will be Unhealthy.

storageConfigurations.description: The storage configurations defined for the runtime environment.

subnetIds.description: The unique identifiers of the subnets assigned to this runtime environment.

tags.description: The tags defined for this runtime environment.

vpcId.description: The unique identifier for the VPC used with this runtime environment.

nextToken.description: If there are more items to return, this contains a token that is passed to a subsequent call to this operation to retrieve the next set of items.

creationTime.description: The timestamp when the application version was created.

nextToken.description: If there are more items to return, this contains a token that is passed to a subsequent call to this operation to retrieve the next set of items.

fileBatchJobDefinition.description: Specifies a file containing a batch job definition.

fileName.description: The name of the file containing the batch job definition.

folderPath.description: The path to the file containing the batch job definition.

scriptBatchJobDefinition.description: A script containing a batch job definition.

scriptName.description: The name of the script containing the batch job definition.

batchJobExecutions.description: Returns a list of batch job executions for an application.

nextToken.description: A pagination token that's returned when the response doesn't contain all batch job executions.

applicationId.description: The unique identifier of the application that hosts this batch job.

batchJobIdentifier.description: The unique identifier of this batch job.

fileBatchJobIdentifier.description: Specifies a file associated with a specific batch job.

fileName.description: The file name for the batch job identifier.

folderPath.description: The relative path to the file name for the batch job identifier.

restartBatchJobIdentifier.description: Specifies the required information for restart, including executionId and JobStepRestartMarker.

executionId.description: The executionId from the StartBatchJob response when the job ran for the first time.

jobStepRestartMarker.description: The step/procedure step information for a restart batch job operation.

fromProcStep.description: The procedure step name that a batch job was restarted from.

fromStep.description: The step name that a batch job was restarted from.

skip.description: The step-level checkpoint timestamp (creation or last modification) for an Amazon Web Services Blu Age application batch job.

stepCheckpoint.description: Skip selected step and issue a restart from immediate successor step for an Amazon Web Services Blu Age application batch job.

toProcStep.description: The procedure step name that a batch job was restarted to.

toStep.description: The step name that a batch job was restarted to.

s3BatchJobIdentifier.description: Specifies an Amazon S3 location that identifies the batch jobs that you want to run. Use this identifier to run ad hoc batch jobs.

bucket.description: The Amazon S3 bucket that contains the batch job definitions.

identifier.description: Identifies the batch job definition. This identifier can also point to any batch job definition that already exists in the application or to one of the batch job definitions within the directory that is specified in keyPrefix.

fileName.description: The name of the file that contains the batch job definition.

scriptName.description: The name of the script that contains the batch job definition.

keyPrefix.description: The key prefix that specifies the path to the folder in the S3 bucket that has the batch job definitions.

scriptBatchJobIdentifier.description: A batch job identifier in which the batch job to run is identified by the script name.

scriptName.description: The name of the script containing the batch job definition.

endTime.description: The timestamp when this batch job execution ended.

executionId.description: The unique identifier of this execution of the batch job.

jobId.description: The unique identifier of a particular batch job.

jobName.description: The name of a particular batch job.

jobType.description: The type of a particular batch job execution.

returnCode.description: The batch job return code from either the Blu Age or Micro Focus runtime engines. For more information, see Batch return codes in the IBM WebSphere Application Server documentation.

startTime.description: The timestamp when a particular batch job execution started.

status.description: The status of a particular batch job execution.

batchJobSteps.description: Returns all the batch job steps and related information for a batch job that previously ran.

stepCheckpoint.description: A registered step-level checkpoint identifier that can be used for restarting an Amazon Web Services Blu Age application batch job.

stepCheckpointStatus.description: The step-level checkpoint status for an Amazon Web Services Blu Age application batch job.

stepCheckpointTime.description: The step-level checkpoint status for an Amazon Web Services Blu Age application batch job.

stepRestartable.description: Specifies if a step can be restarted or not.

nextToken.description: If there are more items to return, this contains a token that is passed to a subsequent call to this operation to retrieve the next set of items.

status.description: The status of the data set export task.

statusReason.description: If dataset exports failed, the failure reason will show here.

summary.description: A summary of the data set export task.

failed.description: The number of data set exports that have failed.

inProgress.description: The number of data set exports that are in progress.

pending.description: The number of data set exports that are pending.

succeeded.description: The number of data set exports that have succeeded.

total.description: The total number of data set exports.

taskId.description: The identifier of the data set export task.

nextToken.description: If there are more items to return, this contains a token that is passed to a subsequent call to this operation to retrieve the next set of items.

status.description: The status of the data set import task.

statusReason.description: If dataset import failed, the failure reason will show here.

summary.description: A summary of the data set import task.

failed.description: The number of data set imports that have failed.

inProgress.description: The number of data set imports that are in progress.

pending.description: The number of data set imports that are pending.

succeeded.description: The number of data set imports that have succeeded.

total.description: The total number of data set imports.

taskId.description: The identifier of the data set import task.

dataSets.description: The list of data sets, containing information including the creation time, the data set name, the data set organization, the data set format, and the last time the data set was referenced or updated.

nextToken.description: If there are more items to return, this contains a token that is passed to a subsequent call to this operation to retrieve the next set of items.

creationTime.description: The timestamp when the data set was created.

dataSetOrg.description: The type of data set. The only supported value is VSAM.

lastReferencedTime.description: The last time the data set was referenced.

lastUpdatedTime.description: The last time the data set was updated.

deployments.description: The list of deployments that is returned.

nextToken.description: If there are more items to return, this contains a token that is passed to a subsequent call to this operation to retrieve the next set of items.

creationTime.description: The timestamp when the deployment was created.

environmentId.description: The unique identifier of the runtime environment.

executionId.description: The unique identifier of this execution of the batch job.

environmentId.description: The unique identifier of the runtime environment that was updated.
